title: caruelina martiana
intro: Caruelina martiana, also known as Martiana's tree or Caruelina, is a plant species that belongs to the family Annonaceae and is native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions such as fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Caruelina martiana has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and swelling, and supporting the immune system. The plant's leaves and bark are commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
intro_study: According to "Pharmacognosy research", Caruelina Martiana (also known as jatobá) has the barks with the highest total phenolic and flavonoid contents.
